data.title: Vietnam shutting down 86M bank accounts due to biometrics. This move highlights importance of Bitcoin adoption.
data.description: Vietnam is said to be closing a total of 86 million bank accounts, a move that has prompted crypto advocates to highlight the importance of holding Bitcoin. The closure of these accounts has drawn attention to the benefits of utilizing decentralized cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin in light of potential restrictions on traditional banking systems.

data.title: DEME orders new Offshore Construction Vessel to boost cable installation capacity for global offshore wind market.
data.description: DEME is increasing its fleet by adding a new Offshore Construction Vessel (OCV) to enhance its subsea cable installation capabilities for the growing global offshore wind sector. This versatile vessel will have the capacity to perform trenching, burial, and cable-laying tasks, augmenting DEME's current fleet such as the Living Stone and Viking Neptun. This expansion will bolster DEME's ability to meet the demands of the offshore wind market.

data.title: JERAYGO (aprocitentan) approved in Switzerland by Swissmedic for resistant hypertension, dosage at 12.5 mg daily, can increase to 25 mg if needed for BP control.
data.description: In Allschwil, Switzerland, on September 19, 2025, Idorsia Ltd (SIX: IDIA) revealed that Swissmedic approved the marketing of JERAYGO ™ (aprocitentan) for addressing resistant hypertension in adult patients when combined with a minimum of three antihypertensive medications. The prescribed initial dose stands at 12.5 mg taken orally once daily. In cases where patients can tolerate the initial dosage, and there is a requirement for more stringent blood pressure control, the dose can be increased to 25 mg per day.
Trump claims UK PM committed to Russian oil ban and sanctions.

data.title: US President's Working Group suggests new regulations for crypto, including market structure and AML measures. #cryptocurrency
data.description: A new framework for regulating cryptocurrencies has been released by the U.S. President's Working Group on Digital Asset Markets. The framework covers key areas such as market structure, stablecoins, and anti-money laundering measures. This comprehensive approach aims to address the growing digital asset market. The guidelines set forth by the group will impact numerous stakeholders in the cryptocurrency industry. Key aspects include regulations surrounding stablecoins and measures to combat money laundering. This move comes amidst increasing interest in cryptocurrencies, with the framework expected to streamline regulations in this evolving sector.
